item.title: ENORMOUS tree stump, 27th June
item.description:
This tree stump is very hazardous. Some one on a bike could hit it and injure themselves badly because it is obscured by the overgrown vegetation. I reported to the council and all they did was put some yellow hazard tape on it. If there is an injury or accident relating to this, the council will be responsible for failing in their duty of care to remove instructions on the highway. It is COUNCIL responsibility because it is NOT an A road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ivy Road, Brockley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead Fox hit by car in middle of road, 27th June
item.description:
Looks like the fox had been hit by a car not too long ago. Fox cubs have been seen near the dead body checking it out. People shouldn’t be driving so recklessly in a suburban area.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Village Way, Eden Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 8-22 Hoyland Close, 27th June
item.description:
We found the graffiti in top floor in communal area please fix it.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 8-23, Hoyland Close, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Formal Escalation – Ongoing Drainage and Flooding Issues (Ref: 7052073), 27th June
item.description:
Dear Sir/Madam, I am writing to formally escalate ongoing drainage, flooding and blocked sewage issues affecting the area around Yarnton Way, which I and other residents have been reporting for several years. The issue is recurring and predictable. Every time there is significant rainfall, the drains become overwhelmed, surface water accumulates, and the area experiences flooding and drainage problems. In addition, there are ongoing concerns regarding blocked sewage drains, which appear unable to cope during periods of heavy rain. Despite multiple reports to various organisations, including Peabody, Thames Water and the London Borough of Bexley, the matter remains unresolved. Responsibility continues to be passed between organisations, while the underlying issue persists year after year. Most recently, I reported the matter under reference 7052073 on 29 January 2025. Since then, I have continued to witness the same issues occurring whenever it rains. I have photographic and video evidence documenting the flooding, drainage failures and blocked drains. I have also personally observed Thames Water vehicles attending the area on a number of occasions. However, these visits often appear brief, with vehicles remaining on site for a short period before leaving, after which the problem inevitably returns during the next period of heavy rainfall. At this stage, I am seeking clear accountability rather than further redirection between organisations. I therefore request that each organisation copied into this email confirms: • Whether it accepts responsibility for any aspect of the drainage or sewage infrastructure involved; • What investigations have been carried out to date; • What findings have been made from those investigations; • What remedial works have been undertaken; • If responsibility is disputed, the evidence supporting that position; and • What specific actions will now be taken to deliver a permanent resolution. As a resident, it is unreasonable to continue reporting the same issue over a period of years without receiving a clear explanation or lasting solution. The repeated flooding and apparent drainage failures suggest that the underlying cause has either not been identified or has not been adequately addressed. If this matter cannot be resolved through the relevant complaints procedures, I intend to escalate it to the appropriate Ombudsman services and seek the support of local councillors and my Member of Parliament. I would be grateful for a substantive response within 20 working days. Kind regards, Andrew Offe
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Harrow Manorway, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road surfacing debris left in residence parking bays, 27th June
item.description:
Work was done over two days 23/24 June to resurface part of Lion Road. Mechanical equipment was parked in the residence parking bay & tarmac debris has not been swept up, the pavements are also messy. It all needs a good sweep up, something the contractors didn’t do before leaving the site. Road closed signage was also left behind leaving drivers believing the road was still closed when it clearly wasn’t. I note that the said signage is now in someone’s front garden!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 49, Lion Road, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown Tree, 26th June
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Carstairs Road, Southend, Greater London
